methods.description: Process for preparing paleosol sediments and extracting lipids for analysis.

steps.description: Paleosol sediments were gently washed with methanol to remove storage and handling contamination, freeze-dried (LABCONCO 4.5), and powdered using a ball mill (Spex 8000).
steps.searchable_summary: wash sediments with methanol, freeze-dry, and powder using ball mill

steps.description: Lipids were extracted from sediments using Soxhlet extraction (9:1 dichloromethane to methanol, 24 hrs) and separated via Accelerated Solvent Extraction (Dionox ASE 200) as per Magill et al., 2015.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Soxhlet extraction and accelerated solvent extraction on sediments

steps.description: Urea adduction was used on the saturated fraction to remove branched molecules from straight chains.
steps.searchable_summary: apply urea adduction to remove branched molecules

steps.description: To evaluate potential laboratory contamination blanks were ran for each processing step, molecules of interest in this study were below detection limits.
steps.searchable_summary: run blanks to check for laboratory contamination

methods.description: Quantification of n-alkanes using gas chromatography and mass spectrometry.

steps.description: n-Alkanes were quantified via GC-FID-MS with Thermo Trace 1310 GC coupled to a split flame ionization detector and a Thermo ISQ LT Single Quadrapole mass selection detector.
Samples were injected in splitless mode onto a 30m Rxi-5HT fused-silica column (250 µm × 0.25 µm) via a TriPlus RSH autosampler.
steps.searchable_summary: inject samples in splitless mode onto GC column

steps.description: GC temperature was programmed to 60°C for 1.5 min then ramped to 140°C at 15 °C·min −1 for 5 min, then ramped to 320°C 6°C·min −1 and held for 10 minutes.
Compounds were identified using fragmentation patterns and retention times matched to a C 7 -C 40 n-alkane standard suite (Supelco).
steps.searchable_summary: identify compounds using fragmentation patterns and retention times

steps.description: Samples were quantified from FID using a 5-point calibration curve for the C 7 -C 40 n-alkane standard suite (Supelco).
steps.searchable_summary: quantify samples using FID and calibration curve

methods.description: Characterization of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) using gas chromatography-mass spectrometry.

steps.description: PAHs were characterized by GC-MS with a Hewlett-Packard 6890 series GC and Hewlett-Packard 5973 mass selective detector.
Samples were injected in splitless mode onto a 30m HP-5 fused-silica column (250 µm × 0.25 µm) via a Hewlett-Packard 7683 series autosampler.
steps.searchable_summary: inject samples in splitless mode onto GC column

steps.description: GC temperature was programmed to 40°C for 1 min, then ramped to 320°C at 6.5°C·min −1.
steps.searchable_summary: program GC temperature for PAH analysis

steps.description: PAHs were quantified in selective ion monitoring mode (SIM) using a 5 point calibration curve for a 17 PAH authentic standard suite (Sigma Aldrich & Certiprep).
steps.searchable_summary: quantify PAHs using SIM and calibration curve

methods.description: Measurement of carbon isotopes in n-alkanes and bulk total organic carbon using isotope-ratio-monitoring gas chromatography-mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Carbon isotopes of n-alkanes were measured using isotope-ratio-monitoring gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (irmGCMS) (Varian 3400 GC connected to Thermo MAT 252).
Samples were run in duplicate. Error was propagated using the methods described by Polissar and D'Andrea (2014) applied for carbon isotopes.
steps.searchable_summary: run samples in duplicate and propagate error

steps.description: Reference standards were used for quality assurance every three samples (Schimmelman A6).
steps.searchable_summary: use reference standards for quality assurance

steps.description: Bulk total organic carbon δ 13 C were measured after decarbonation of ground paleosols with excess 1 N hydrochloric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: measure bulk total organic carbon δ 13 C after decarbonation
outputs: bulk total organic carbon δ 13 C data

steps.description: Replicate measurements of these standards were used throughout sample runs to ensure accuracy (±0.2‰, n=24; 1σ ) and precision (±0.2‰, n=24; 1σ).
steps.searchable_summary: use replicate measurements for accuracy and precision
